title: Medusanthera glabra
taxonomy: Medusanthera glabra belongs to the family Gesneriaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the African violet family or the gesneriad family. The genus Medusanthera is a part of this family, and it is native to the tropical regions of Africa. Medusanthera glabra is a species within this genus, and its classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. The classification of Medusanthera glabra provides insight into its evolutionary relationships and its place within the plant kingdom.

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
